name: Hoe (SS-258)
events: 26 Feb 1943 USS Hoe (Lt.Cdr. V.B. McCrea, USN) departed New London, Connecticut and arrived at Newport, Rhode Island for special torpedo firing tests.
10 Mar 1943 Having completed her special torpedo firing tests, USS Hoe (Lt.Cdr. V.B. McCrea, USN), returned to New London from Newport, Rhode Island.
14 Mar 1943 USS Hoe (Lt.Cdr. V.B. McCrea, USN) departed New London, Connecticut for the Portsmouth Navy Yard for repairs.
11 Apr 1943 Having completed her repairs, USS Hoe (Lt.Cdr. V.B. McCrea, USN), arrived at New London, Connecticut from the Portsmouth Navy Yard.
20 Apr 1943 USS Hoe (Lt.Cdr. V.B. McCrea, USN) departed New London, Connecticut for the Panama Canal Zone.
1 May 1943 USS Hoe (Lt.Cdr. V.B. McCrea, USN) transited the Panama Canal.
27 May 1943 USS Hoe (Lt.Cdr. V.B. McCrea) departed from Pearl Harbor for her 1st war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the Mariana Islands area.
11 Jul 1943 USS Hoe (Lt.Cdr. V.B. McCrea) ended her 1st war patrol at Pearl Harbor.
21 Aug 1943 USS Hoe (Lt.Cdr. V.B. McCrea) departed from Pearl Harbor for her 2nd war patrol. She was ordered to patrol off Truk.
18 Oct 1943 USS Hoe (Lt.Cdr. V.B. McCrea) ended her 2nd war patrol at Pearl Harbor.

26 Jan 1944 After extensive repairs to her main engines USS Hoe (Lt.Cdr. V.B. McCrea) departed from Pearl Harbor for her 3rd war patrol. She was ordered to patrol south of the Philippines between Mindanao and Halmahera.

25 Feb 1944 While operating south of Davao Gulf, Philippines USS Hoe (Lt.Cdr. V.B. McCrea) torpedoed and sank the Japanese fleet tanker Nissho Maru (10526 GRT) in position 05°50'N, 126°00'E and torpedoed and damaged the Japanese fleet tanker Kyokuto Maru (10051 GRT, offsite link) in position 05°38'N, 126°00'E.
5 Mar 1944 USS Hoe (Lt.Cdr. V.B. McCrea) ended her 3rd war patrol at Fremantle, Australia.
4 Apr 1944 USS Hoe (Lt.Cdr. V.B. McCrea) departed from Fremantle for her 4h war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the South China Sea.

8 May 1944 USS Hoe (Lt.Cdr. V.B. McCrea) torpedoed and damaged the Japanese frigate Sado (870 tons, offsite link) and the Japanese army tanker Akane Maru (10241 GRT) in the South China Sea west of Luzon Strait in position 19°22'N, 120°13'E.
2 Jun 1944 USS Hoe (Lt.Cdr. V.B. McCrea) ended her 4th war patrol at Fremantle.

22 Jun 1944 HMAS Adelaide (A/Capt. J.C.D. Esdaile, OBE, RAN), HMAS Horsham (T/A/Lt.Cdr. W.H. Newby, RANR(S)) and USS Isabel (Lt. E.G. Zacher, USNR) conducted exercises off Fremantle with the submarines USS Cod (T/Cdr. J.A. Adkins, USN), USS Hoe (T/Cdr. V.B. McCrea, USN) and USS Robalo (T/Lt.Cdr. M.M. Kimmel, USN). (1)
29 Jun 1944 USS Hoe (Lt.Cdr. V.B. McCrea) departed from Fremantle for her 5th war patrol. Once again she was ordered to patrol in the South China Sea.
23 Aug 1944 USS Hoe (Lt.Cdr. V.B. McCrea) ended her 5th war patrol at Fremantle.
15 Sep 1944 USS Hoe (Lt.Cdr. V.B. McCrea) departed from Fremantle for her 6th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol southwest of Lingayen Gulf.

15 Sep 1944 HMAS Adelaide (A/Capt. L.E. Tozer, RAN) conducted gunnery exercises off Fremantle. On completion of which she joined USS Isabel (Lt. E.G. Zacher, USNR) for conducted exercises with the submarines USS Cod (T/Cdr. J.A. Adkins, USN), USS Bluegill (T/Cdr. E.L. Barr, Jr., USN) and USS Hoe (T/Cdr. V.B. McCrea, USN). (2)

22 Sep 1944 At 1144 I/22, in position 18°02'S, 115°55'E, USS Hardhead (T/Cdr. F. McMaster, USN) sighted what appeared to be a submarine through the high periscope on bearing 143° at a distance of about 15 miles. USS Hardhead turned away.
At 1158 I/22, the submarine disappeared over the horizon. Changed course to the eastward and commenced trailing.
At 1258 I/22, sight was regained on the other submarine. The periscope shears appeared to the USN-type.

At 1327 I/22, the other submarine slowed down so did the same. Attempted to exchange recognition signals with the SJ radar but was not successful. The other submarine appeared to be on a course of 060°.
At 1355 I/22, the other submarine suddenly disappeared most likely it had dived. Hardhead resumed her original course.
The submarine encountered was most likely the northbound USS Hoe (T/Cdr. V.B. McCrea, USN). (3)

8 Oct 1944 USS Hoe (Lt.Cdr. V.B. McCrea) torpedoed and sank the Japanese troop transport Kohoku Maru (2578 GRT) and torpedoed and damaged the Japanese corvette Kaibokan 8 (940 tons, offsite link) in the South China Sea east of Hainan in position 18°32'N, 116°13'E.
22 Oct 1944 USS Hoe (Lt.Cdr. V.B. McCrea) ended her 6th war patrol at Fremantle.
23 Nov 1944 USS Hoe (Lt.Cdr. M.P. Refo III) departed from Fremantle for her 7th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the Java Sea.
3 Jan 1945 USS Hoe (Lt.Cdr. M.P. Refo III) ended her 7th war patrol at Fremantle.
8 Feb 1945 USS Hoe (Lt.Cdr. M.P. Refo III) departed from Fremantle for her 8th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the South China Sea.

18 Feb 1945 At 2030 H/18, in Karimata Strait, USS Hawkbill (T/Cdr. W. Scanland, Jr., USN) exchanged calls with USS Hoe (T/Lt.Cdr. M.P. Refo 3rd, USN) which was also proceeding through the Strait northbound.
USS Hoe reported the encounter at 2035 H/18 in position 01°58'S, 108°53'E. (4)
23 Feb 1945 USS Hoe (Lt.Cdr. M.P. Refo III) collides with her sister ship USS Flounder 66 feet beneath the surface. Fortunately neither submarine was seriously damaged.
25 Feb 1945 USS Hoe (Lt.Cdr. M.P. Refo III) torpedoed and sank the Japanese frigate Shonan (940 tons, offsite link) south of Hainan Island in position 17°05'N, 110°05'E.
6 Mar 1945 USS Hoe (Lt.Cdr. M.P. Refo III) ended her 8th war patrol at Pearl Harbor. He is now sent to the Mare Island Navy Yard for a major overhaul.

31 Jul 1945 USS Bowfin (Cdr. A.K. Tyree, USN) conducted exercises off Pearl Harbor together with USS Pruitt (Lt. D.B. Harby, USNR), USS Florikan (Lt. D. Hima, USN) and USS Hoe (Lt.Cdr. M.P. Refo III, USN).
